title: Tanker report - Week 43
summary:
Clean
LR2
LR's in the MEG look to have lost steam this week following last week's moderate firming. The 75Kt MEG/Japan TC1 index has remained stable with enough activity for it to climb from WS170 to WS173. Similarly, a 90kt MEG/UK-Continent TC20 run freight has hovered around the $4.4m level all week.

children.title: Clean
children.summary:
LR2
LR's in the MEG look to have lost steam this week following last week's moderate firming. The 75Kt MEG/Japan TC1 index has remained stable with enough activity for it to climb from WS170 to WS173.

children.title: VLCC
children.summary: The market steadied this week with rates generally slighter firmer except in the US Gulf arena. The rate for 270,000 mt Middle East Gulf to China rose two points to WS56.38 corresponding to a daily round-trip TCE of $31,477 basis the Baltic Exchange's vessel description.

children.title: Suezmax
children.summary: Suezmaxes in West Africa have had another good week with rates climbing further. The rate for 130,000 mt Nigeria/UK Continent rose 27 points to WS145 (a daily round-trip TCE of $65,933).

children.title: Aframax
children.summary: In the North Sea, the rate for the 80,000 mt Cross-UK Continent route rocketed another 40 points to WS213.57 (showing a round-trip daily TCE of $103,055 basis Hound Point to Wilhelmshaven). In the Mediterranean market, the rate for 80,000 mt Cross-Mediterranean firmed by another six points to WS220.28 (basis Ceyhan to Lavera showing a daily round trip TCE of $79,117).
